texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Laboratory System has interaction with almost all other Decentralized Hospital Computer Program (DHCP) packages. Because of this interaction, as well as the patient data maintained in the Laboratory files, security is a real and necessary concern.
texts.text: The Laboratory System has interaction with almost all other Decentralized Hospital Computer Program (DHCP) packages. Because of this interaction, as well as the patient data maintained in the Laboratory files, security is a real and necessary concern.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Laboratory System security is maintained by use of Laboratory security keys, Laboratory file security, and proper menu assignments. This guide will discuss each element of security management and give guidelines for the assignment of access to the Laboratory System.
texts.text: Laboratory System security is maintained by use of Laboratory security keys, Laboratory file security, and proper menu assignments. This guide will discuss each element of security management and give guidelines for the assignment of access to the Laboratory System.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each user of the Lab system must have the appropriate “keys” assigned to him before he can access the system. File #19.1 (SECURITY KEY) contains the key names, a short description, and a list of the holders. You will need to enter the users’ names under each of the appropriate key names. Following is a list of the Lab keys:
texts.text: Each user of the Lab system must have the appropriate “keys” assigned to him before he can access the system. File #19.1 (SECURITY KEY) contains the key names, a short description, and a list of the holders. You will need to enter the users’ names under each of the appropriate key names. Following is a list of the Lab keys:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It should be noted that these keys are used as locks for particular options, and are also checked elsewhere. Some routines check for user security keys.
texts.text: It should be noted that these keys are used as locks for particular options, and are also checked elsewhere. Some routines check for user security keys.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LRAPSUPER	Anyone allowed to use the Anatomic Pathology Supervisor Menu and edit SNOMED codes
texts.text: LRAPSUPER	Anyone allowed to use the Anatomic Pathology Supervisor Menu and edit SNOMED codes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LRBLSUPER	For Blood Bank supervisory level decisions
texts.text: LRBLSUPER	For Blood Bank supervisory level decisions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LRVERIFY	Anyone who is authorized to verify lab results
texts.text: LRVERIFY	Anyone who is authorized to verify lab results

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LRPHSUPER	Supervisor of the phlebotomy collection team
texts.text: LRPHSUPER	Supervisor of the phlebotomy collection team

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Any combination of the security levels may be used, as deemed appropriate by the Laboratory.
texts.text: Any combination of the security levels may be used, as deemed appropriate by the Laboratory.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Two additional fields have been added to the software package to allow other ancillary laboratories (not within Laboratory Service) to use this module for accessioning, verification, and reporting of their data:
texts.text: Two additional fields have been added to the software package to allow other ancillary laboratories (not within Laboratory Service) to use this module for accessioning, verification, and reporting of their data:

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
1.	File #68 (Accession Area), field: User Access Authorization
This field points to File #19.1, Security Keys. If there is an entry in this field, only users having the security key for this accession area will be able to use the verification options for data entry or modification of data. This will not affect users’ ability to accession tests for the Accession Area.
texts.text:
1.	File #68 (Accession Area), field: User Access Authorization
This field points to File #19.1, Security Keys. If there is an entry in this field, only users having the security key for this accession area will be able to use the verification options for data entry or modification of data. This will not affect users’ ability to accession tests for the Accession Area.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: :	You will need to contact your site manager if new keys need to be established. The key must exist before it can be assigned. If users NOT having the Access Authorization selects the locked Accession Area, the system echoes “??” and allows them to select again.
texts.text: :	You will need to contact your site manager if new keys need to be established. The key must exist before it can be assigned. If users NOT having the Access Authorization selects the locked Accession Area, the system echoes “??” and allows them to select again.

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
2.	File #68.2 (Load/Worklist), field: User Access Authorization
A site may also select to prevent the building and unloading of load/work lists from users outside of the Accession Area. An entry in this field will give this additional security.  This field also points to the File #19.1.
texts.text:
2.	File #68.2 (Load/Worklist), field: User Access Authorization
A site may also select to prevent the building and unloading of load/work lists from users outside of the Accession Area. An entry in this field will give this additional security.  This field also points to the File #19.1.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If these fields have null entries, any user may access the Accession Area and the Load/Worklists.
texts.text: If these fields have null entries, any user may access the Accession Area and the Load/Worklists.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Blood Bank Module requires only the LRBLOODBANK key to access the majority of the options. The LRBLSUPER key is, however, necessary to access all of the options in the Supervisor’s Menu, as well as to release incompatible blood using the Disposition-Relocation [LRBLIDR] option in the Inventory Menu.
texts.text: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Blood Bank Module requires only the LRBLOODBANK key to access the majority of the options. The LRBLSUPER key is, however, necessary to access all of the options in the Supervisor’s Menu, as well as to release incompatible blood using the Disposition-Relocation [LRBLIDR] option in the Inventory Menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since the various options, excluding those in the Supervisor’s menu, do not require specific security keys, regulating access to the options must be accomplished via strict menu management. Menus must be constructed at each site, tailored to the actual needs of the individuals using the module.
texts.text: Since the various options, excluding those in the Supervisor’s menu, do not require specific security keys, regulating access to the options must be accomplished via strict menu management. Menus must be constructed at each site, tailored to the actual needs of the individuals using the module.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Anatomic Pathology Module requires several specific keys to access the appropriate Accession Area
texts.text: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Anatomic Pathology Module requires several specific keys to access the appropriate Accession Area

texts.label: text
texts.orig: and the options that should be limited to Supervisory grade or higher level personnel.
texts.text: and the options that should be limited to Supervisory grade or higher level personnel.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: LRAPSUPER	Anyone allowed to use the Anatomic Pathology Supervisor Menu and edit SNOMED codes
texts.text: LRAPSUPER	Anyone allowed to use the Anatomic Pathology Supervisor Menu and edit SNOMED codes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Since the various options, excluding those in the Supervisor’s menu, do not require specific security keys once you have the appropriate Accession Area keys, regulating access to the options must be accomplished via strict menu management.
texts.text: Since the various options, excluding those in the Supervisor’s menu, do not require specific security keys once you have the appropriate Accession Area keys, regulating access to the options must be accomplished via strict menu management.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Microbiology Module requires only the LRMICRO key to access the majority of the options and the LRMIVERIFY key to release results. The LRSUPER key is necessary to access the options in the Microbiology Supervisor’s Menu.
texts.text: In addition to the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys, the Microbiology Module requires only the LRMICRO key to access the majority of the options and the LRMIVERIFY key to release results. The LRSUPER key is necessary to access the options in the Microbiology Supervisor’s Menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Chemistry subscript Module requires the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys to access the majority of the options. The LRSUPER key is necessary to access the options in the Supervisor’s Menu.
texts.text: The Chemistry subscript Module requires the LRLAB and LRVERIFY security keys to access the majority of the options. The LRSUPER key is necessary to access the options in the Supervisor’s Menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The phlebotomy section requires  the LRLAB and LRPHMAN keys to access the necessary options. The LRPHSUPER key is, however, necessary to access the options needed by the Phlebotomy supervisor.
texts.text: The phlebotomy section requires  the LRLAB and LRPHMAN keys to access the necessary options. The LRPHSUPER key is, however, necessary to access the options needed by the Phlebotomy supervisor.
